title: 11 Herbal Teas For Chapped Lips
intro_desc: As a skier, you're exposed to harsh winter conditions - cold winds, dry air, and intense sunlight. These elements can dry out your skin, including your lips, leading to chapped and cracked lips. Chapped lips can be painful, and it's not just about the physical discomfort. It can also affect your mood and overall experience on the slopes. Imagine skiing down a beautiful mountain, but instead of enjoying the thrill, you're worried about how your lips feel. Chapped lips can also lead to bleeding and infections, which can keep you from skiing as often as you'd like. When your lips are chapped, it's hard to enjoy the simple things, like sipping hot chocolate or taking in the breathtaking views.  As a skier, you need something that can help soothe and protect your lips. That's where herbal teas come in. Some herbal teas have natural moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation. These teas can be used as a lip balm or added to your bath water for a relaxing soak. They can help to soften and heal chapped lips, so you can get back to what matters most - hitting the slopes.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chapped lips. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have soothing properties that can keep your lips moisturized. These teas can also help reduce dryness and inflammation caused by cold weather or dry air, which can lead to chapped lips.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent chapped lips from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have soothing properties that can keep your lips moisturized. They can also help calm dry skin, which can reduce the risk of chapped lips. This is because herbal teas often contain antioxidants that support skin health.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for chapped lips are generally safe to consume every day. However, some may cause allergic reactions or interact with medications. Look for teas made from soothing herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, or peppermint. Start with small amounts and observe how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for chapped lips can start working within a few days to a week. Some people notice improvements in lip moisture and smoothness after 2-3 days of regular drinking, but it might take up to a week for the full benefits to show.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for chapped lips can be beneficial at night before bed. Many herbs, such as aloe vera and chamomile, have soothing properties that can calm and moisturize dry, chapped lips while you sleep. This allows the tea's benefits to work overnight, helping to heal and soften your lips by morning.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, but this is rare. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and ginger, have properties that might affect how your body uses certain medications. If you're taking prescription medications for chapped lips, it's best to check the labels of any herbal teas you drink.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some ingredients can affect your baby's health. If you want to try a herbal tea for chapped lips, choose one with well-known and safe ingredients like aloe vera or chamomile. Be sure to check the ingredients and follow any instructions carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing remedy for chapped lips, but while breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbs can pass into breast milk, affecting the baby. Look for teas that are known to be safe during breastfeeding, like peppermint or chamomile. Always check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts to minimize any potential risks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas contains flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and heal chapped lips. The tea's high concentration of flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, helps reduce inflammation and irritation, while its antioxidant properties combat free radicals that can damage lip tissue. The tea's carotenoids, including beta-carotene, also promote collagen production, which can help repair and regenerate damaged lip cells. To use Calendula officinalis tea for chapped lips, you can steep 1 teaspoon of dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool. Soak a cotton swab in the tea and gently apply it to your chapped lips 2-3 times a day. As the tea's active compounds are absorbed into your skin, you can expect to see a reduction in inflammation and discomfort, followed by an improvement in lip texture and appearance. The tea's moisturizing properties can help lock in moisture, leaving your lips feeling soft and supple.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains compounds like alkylamides, phenolic acids, and glycosides that help soothe and heal dry, chapped lips. These properties work by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production, which helps to repair and firm up the skin on your lips. To use Echinacea purpurea teas for chapped lips, you can make a warm tea by steeping a teaspoon of dried Echinacea roots in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Then, let it cool down before applying it to your lips using a cotton swab or a clean cloth. You can also mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to create a soothing lip balm.  When you apply the tea or balm to your chapped lips, you can expect to see a reduction in dryness and discomfort within a few hours. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea may help to reduce redness and swelling, while the antioxidants can help to protect your skin from further damage. With regular use, you may find that your lips become softer, smoother, and more resilient to environmental stressors. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea teas may not completely cure chapped lips, especially if they are caused by underlying conditions like eczema or vitamin deficiencies. It's always a good idea to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new herbal remedy, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is known for its soothing properties that can help to alleviate dry and chapped lips. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling in the lips, while also locking in moisture to prevent further dryness. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas also contains antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote healthy skin cell growth. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for chapped lips, try steeping a tea bag in hot water, letting it cool, and then applying it directly to the affected area with a cotton swab. Leave the tea on for 10-15 minutes to allow the glycyrrhizin to penetrate the skin and provide its soothing benefits. After removing the tea, rinse your lips with cold water and apply a lip balm or moisturizer to lock in the moisture. With regular use, you may start to notice a decrease in lip dryness and inflammation, and your lips may start to feel softer and more comfortable. You may also notice an improvement in the overall appearance of your lips, with a reduction in fine lines and wrinkles.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas contains several properties and constituents that help with chapped lips, including vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium, magnesium, and potassium. These nutrients help to moisturize and soothe dry, cracked skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Aloe barbadensis also contains a gel-like substance called mucilage, which creates a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and shielding it from further irritation.  To use Aloe barbadensis teas for chapped lips, start by steeping a teaspoon of dried Aloe barbadensis leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool, then strain and use the liquid as a lip balm or apply it directly to the affected area. You can also add a few drops of honey or coconut oil to enhance its moisturizing properties.  As you use Aloe barbadensis teas, you can expect to see improvements in the texture and appearance of your lips. The inflammation and redness should decrease, and the dryness should start to heal. Your lips may feel softer and more supple, and they may look smoother and less cracked. With regular use, you can help to prevent future chapping and promote a healthier, more balanced lip environment.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor that help with chapped lips. These ingredients have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. The antioxidant properties in rosmarinic acid can also protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin regeneration.  To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas for chapped lips, you can make a tea by steeping dried rosemary leaves in hot water. Let it cool down before applying it to your lips with a cotton swab. Repeat this process a few times a day. You can also add a few drops of honey or aloe vera gel to the tea for extra moisturizing benefits.  As you apply the tea to your chapped lips, you may feel a soothing sensation as the anti-inflammatory properties start to work. The tea may reduce redness and swelling, allowing your skin to heal faster. With regular use, you may notice an improvement in the texture and appearance of your lips, becoming smoother and healthier-looking. However, if your chapped lips are severe, it's best to consult a healthcare professional for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm chapped lips. The main constituents, linalool and linalyl acetate, are responsible for its antibacterial and moisturizing effects. When you make a tea from dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and apply it to your lips, the antioxidants help reduce inflammation and protect your skin from environmental stressors.  To use Lavandula angustifolia tea for chapped lips, start by steeping a teaspoon of dried flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool, then soak a cotton ball or a small cloth in the tea. Apply the soaked cotton ball or cloth to your chapped lips for 5-10 minutes, 2-3 times a day. You can also add a few drops of the cooled tea to a small bowl of petroleum jelly or lip balm and apply it to your lips.  As you consistently apply the Lavandula angustifolia tea to your chapped lips, you can expect the inflammation to decrease, and the dryness to subside. Your lips should start to feel softer and more comfortable within a few days of regular use. The antibacterial properties of the tea may also help prevent infection and promote faster healing. With continued use, you may find that your lips become more resilient to environmental stressors and less prone to chapping.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas contains compounds like linalool and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These help to calm and comfort chapped lips, reducing redness and swelling. The tea also contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect and repair damaged skin.  To use Melissa officinalis teas for chapped lips, you can make a tea by steeping a few sprigs in hot water, let it cool, and then apply it to your lips with a cotton swab. You can also add a few drops of the tea to your lip balm or lip gloss for added benefits.  When you apply the tea to your chapped lips, you can expect them to feel soothed and calm, with reduced pain and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and redness, and the antioxidants may help to repair and protect the skin. With regular use, your chapped lips may start to heal faster, and you may notice a reduction in the frequency of dry, chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains compounds like menthol and menthone, which have natural antiseptic and moisturizing properties. These properties help to soothe and hydrate chapped lips by reducing inflammation and locking in moisture. The tea's antibacterial properties also help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause further irritation and dryness. To use Mentha x piperita teas for chapped lips, you can add a few drops of peppermint essential oil to a cup of warm water and let it steep for a few minutes. Then, you can use a cotton swab to apply the cooled tea to your lips, or drink the tea as a mouthwash. You can also add a teaspoon of honey or aloe vera gel to the tea for extra moisturizing benefits. When you apply the tea to your chapped lips, you can expect to feel a soothing and refreshing sensation. The menthol and menthone in the tea can help to numb the area and reduce discomfort. As the tea absorbs into your skin, you may start to notice improvements in the texture and appearance of your lips, with a decrease in dryness and a reduction in the appearance of cracks and fissures. Regular use of Mentha x piperita teas may also help to prevent future episodes of chapped lips by maintaining healthy skin and preventing bacterial growth.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas contains compounds like flavonoids, silymarin, and polysaccharides that help soothe and protect chapped lips. The flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling, while silymarin acts as an antioxidant, preventing further damage to the skin. Polysaccharides, particularly beta-glucans, have been shown to stimulate the production of collagen, helping to repair and regenerate the skin tissue. To use Silybum marianum teas for chapped lips, steep a teaspoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Let it cool, then dip a cotton swab into the tea and gently apply it to the affected area. You can also add a few drops of honey or aloe vera gel to the tea for extra moisturizing properties. When you apply the tea, you can expect to see a reduction in lip inflammation and a decrease in the severity of chapping. The antioxidants and flavonoids may also help to repair and regenerate the skin tissue, promoting healthier-looking lips. However, it's essential to note that Silybum marianum teas may not provide immediate relief, so be patient and consistent with your application. It may take some time to notice the full benefits of this remedy.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis teas contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and heal chapped lips. The tea's high content of catechins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids helps to reduce inflammation and protect the lips from environmental stressors. When applied topically, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can calm dry, irritated skin, and its humectant properties can lock in moisture, helping to repair and hydrate chapped lips.  To use Camellia sinensis teas for chapped lips, try steeping a strong tea and letting it cool. Then, use a cotton swab or a clean cloth to apply the cooled tea directly to the affected area. You can also add a teaspoon of honey or aloe vera gel to the tea for extra moisturizing benefits. As you apply the tea, you may feel a soothing, cooling sensation on your lips. Within a few minutes, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties should start to calm the inflammation, and its humectant properties should begin to lock in moisture, helping to hydrate and repair your chapped lips. As a result, you should start to see a reduction in dryness and irritation, and your lips should begin to feel softer, smoother, and more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Camellia sinensis herbal teas for chapped lips

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe dry, chapped lips. The tea also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The astringent properties of the tea can help reduce swelling and dryness, while its antiseptic properties can prevent infection. To use Eucalyptus globulus tea to treat chapped lips, brew a cup according to the package instructions and let it cool. Soak a cotton ball or swab in the cooled tea and gently apply it to your chapped lips, 2-3 times a day. As you use the tea, you may notice a reduction in dryness and swelling, and your lips may start to feel softer and more comfortable. The tea may also help reduce the appearance of fine lines and cracks on the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for chapped lips
